description:
You are a web-developer who maintains a tiny hand-rolled “firewall” that lives entirely in the user’s home directory.  
A configuration file already exists but it currently contains only a header.  
Your goal is to add a single rule that opens TCP port 8080 for incoming traffic and then produce a very short log confirming the change.

System details that matter to you:

1. Directory: /home/user/firewall  
2. Existing file: /home/user/firewall/rules.conf  
   • Present content (exactly two characters, including the newline):  
     ``#`` followed immediately by a newline (`\n`).  
   • The hash mark represents the header line that must stay at the very top of the file.  
3. Permissions: everything in /home/user/firewall is writable by the current user; no sudo needed.  
4. No other firewall-related files or directories exist.

What you must accomplish through the terminal:

A. Append a new line that reads exactly  
   ALLOW 8080  
   to /home/user/firewall/rules.conf, directly after the existing header line. The file must end with a single newline as usual.

B. After updating the configuration file, create a confirmation log named /home/user/firewall/config-change.log whose content is exactly three lines (each ending in “\n”):

   UPDATE SUCCESSFUL  
   File: /home/user/firewall/rules.conf  
   Added rule: ALLOW 8080  

   The spelling, spacing, capitalisation, and newline placement must match the above block byte-for-byte.  
   • No extra blank lines before, between, or after the three lines.  
   • “ALLOW 8080” must use one space between the word and the number.  
   • The colon character in line 2 is followed by exactly one space.

C. Do not modify or create any other files.  
D. You may use any single terminal command (with appropriate shell redirection or tools) to achieve both file modifications.

Verification conditions that automated tests will check:

1. /home/user/firewall/rules.conf exists.  
2. Its exact byte sequence is:  
   “#\nALLOW 8080\n”  
   (That is: a hash, newline, the text “ALLOW 8080”, newline.)  
3. /home/user/firewall/config-change.log exists and its exact byte sequence is:  
   “UPDATE SUCCESSFUL\nFile: /home/user/firewall/rules.conf\nAdded rule: ALLOW 8080\n”

If all three checks pass, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
Directory /home/user/firewall already exists.
File /home/user/firewall/rules.conf exists with content: "#\n"  (one hash followed by newline)
No other files are present inside /home/user/firewall.

Expected final state after successful task execution:
File /home/user/firewall/rules.conf content:
#\nALLOW 8080\n  (two lines, ends with newline)

File /home/user/firewall/config-change.log content:
UPDATE SUCCESSFUL\nFile: /home/user/firewall/rules.conf\nAdded rule: ALLOW 8080\n

No additional files or directories are created or modified.
